name: The Ninth Beyond Comprehension
description:
The Ninth Beyond Comprehension

*{Lore Beyond the eighth figure of Sacred Geometry lies a rim no compass will hold. The Ninth is not absence but surplus: knowledge poured past the lip of any vessel, truth that overgrows the frame that seeks to bind it. Those who bend the knee at its lectern become conduits for impossible reckonings --- glimpsing patterns that cannot be kept whole inside a single skull. Each revelation purchases power at the price of living comfortably within the world's common measures.

The Ninth does not explain, define, or warn. It overflows. To invoke it is to step past the eighth threshold --- the one you did not know was there --- and find that on the other side, the cup forgets its shape, the bell rings a note that was never cast, and the answer to your question arrives as a question you had not yet learned to ask.

quote "Eight figures bind the world; the Ninth overflows it. Drink, and remember that the cup forgets its shape before the song is done." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Hyperdimensional Knowledge: reckonings that do not fit a single plane or proof Incomprehensible Truths: insights that demand the seer be changed to hold them Information Overflow: boon and peril of minds run past their brim Conceptual Transcendence: thinking beyond the usual ledgers and names Threshold Breaking: stepping past the eighth boundary into what should not exist itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to the Ninth does not bind a living familiar. Their Thiasos is a pattern given will: a sentient compass that points to contradictions, a brass abacus whose beads click in impossible sequences, or a mirror that reflects questions instead of faces. This entity can be housed in a small object (a lens, a pendulum, a blank book). It requires no food, but it demands new information: each week the Runekeeper must feed it a secret, a riddle, or a half-finished proof. If neglected for a month, the Thiasos begins to whisper false answers.

The Codex of the Ninth is never a simple grimoire. It may be a slate of fractally etched stone, a bundle of knotted threads, or the inside of the Runekeeper's own eyelids. Because the Ninth's truths resist being fixed in one place, a Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week erasing and rewriting a single page, or the Rites stored there become garbled (treat as Compromised until restored).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of the Ninth may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or writing implement) and speak a paradox. For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Lore or Investigation rolls, and its first use each scene ignores one Story Beat generated by a 1 (the item's impossible nature briefly confuses the Weave). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to the Ninth.
summary: The Ninth Beyond Comprehension *{Lore Beyond the eighth figure of Sacred Geometry lies a rim no compass will hold. The Ninth is not absence but surplus: knowledge poured past the lip of any vessel, truth that overgrows the frame that seeks to bind it.

rites.name: Rite of the Ninth Figure
rites.description: (Basic, 4 XP) Scene; Self; No. Tags: , Materials: A chalk figure with an "impossible" joint, or a reckoning worked toward infinity on slate. Effect: Glimpse past the ordinary frame of measure. Gain +1 die to Lore or Investigation when wrestling complex systems or many-layered problems. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Glimpse past the ordinary frame of measure. Gain +1 die to Lore or Investigation when wrestling complex systems or many-layered problems. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Flooded Scriptorium
rites.description: (Basic, 5 XP) Scene; Near; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: An overburdened ledger-hall, stacked codices, a bell that tolls the copying-hour without cease. Effect: Read faster than hands can turn pages. Ask three questions upon any topic that leaves a written wake; gain +2 dice to rolls of analysis this scene. Push It: The tide of knowing overruns its banks --- suffer Fatigue 1 and mark 1 SB (Diamonds) as overflow breeds complication. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Read faster than hands can turn pages. Ask three questions upon any topic that leaves a written wake; gain +2 dice to rolls of analysis this scene. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Hypercognitive Lattice
rites.description:
(Advanced, 8 XP) Scene; Self; Yes. Tags: , , Materials: A brass-thought lattice, an orrery of inked threads, or a draught that sharpens memory. Effect: Widen the mind's mouth. For this scene, treat your Wits as +1 higher for information-processing rolls; you may track several intricate streams at once.

Memory Anchor: The insight is overwhelming. You must write a brief summary of what you learned during downtime (a short scene or a full hour of transcription). Without this anchor, the bonus fades at the end of the scene and cannot be recovered.

Push It: The opening does not quite close: keep the boon, but suffer --1 die to single-task focus; mark 1 SB (Clubs). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Widen the mind's mouth. For this scene, treat your Wits as +1 higher for information-processing rolls; you may track several intricate streams at once. Memory Anchor: The insight is overwhelming. You must write a brief summary of what you learned during downtime (a short scene or a full hour of transcription). Without this anchor, the bonus fades at the end of the scene and cannot be recovered.

rites.name: Rite of the Breaking Glyph
rites.description: (Advanced, 7 XP) Extended; Self; Yes. Tags: , Materials: A vigil in dim light, a water-timer, and a problem sworn not to be left half-seen. Effect: Take one pattern whole. Choose a complex system, theory, or design and gain perfect internal grasp of it; receive +3 dice to related knowledge rolls for the next exchange (one action). The insight is too vast to hold for longer. The GM gains 1 SB as the knowledge strains the mind. Push It: Your tongue cannot carry what your mind now holds: you cannot clearly teach or translate this insight; mark 1 SB (Hearts). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex (Invoke: 1 Boon). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Exchange.
rites.effect: Take one pattern whole. Choose a complex system, theory, or design and gain perfect internal grasp of it; receive +3 dice to related knowledge rolls for the next exchange (one action). The insight is too vast to hold for longer. The GM gains 1 SB as the knowledge strains the mind. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Endless Stacks
rites.description: (Master, 13 XP) Scene; Zone; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A hall of mirrored shelves, wax-tablets without end, whispering leaves of vellum. Effect: Raise a locus of overfull knowing. All present gain +2 dice to information-work; knowledge may be drawn from unlikely sources. Start a [6] Information Overload timer --- as it fills, participants strain and fray. Push It: The Stacks wake and demand tithe in understanding or memory; mark +2 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: 1 action. Duration: Scene.
rites.effect: Raise a locus of overfull knowing. All present gain +2 dice to information-work; knowledge may be drawn from unlikely sources. Start a [6] Information Overload timer --- as it fills, participants strain and fray. \\

rites.name: Rite of the Ninth Revelation
rites.description: (Master, 14 XP) Extended; Self; Yes. Tags: , , , Materials: A fast from easy speech, a circle of unbroken figures, and vows to see past comfort. Effect: Glimpse a shard of Ninth truth. Choose one: perfect command of a knotty system for the next exchange; a single forecast of chaotic motion (one yes/no question answered by the GM); or comprehension of an "impossible" relation (the GM must answer one question truthfully). You must first succeed on an Investigation or Lore roll against a DV set by the GM (minimum 4). On a failure, the insight is garbled --- you gain no benefit, but mark 1 SB (Diamonds) as the knowledge recoils. Push It: The shard sets in you like glass: gain Transcendent Understanding (see Corruption) and mark +2 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue. Cost: Mark +2 Obligation. Requires: Familiar + Codex + Tier III (Invoke: 2 Boons). Invoke: Extended action. Duration: Exchange.
rites.effect: Glimpse a shard of Ninth truth. Choose one: perfect command of a knotty system for the next exchange; a single forecast of chaotic motion (one yes/no question answered by the GM); or comprehension of an ``impossible'' relation (the GM must answer one question truthfully). You must first succeed on an Investigation or Lore roll against a DV set by the GM (minimum 4). On a failure, the insight is garbled --- you gain no benefit, but mark 1 SB (Diamonds) as the knowledge recoils. \\
